introSection: Mad Mouse (Japanese: マッドマウス) is a steel sit-down roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer SBF Visa Group located at Rusutsu Resort in Rusutsu, Abuta, Shiribeshi, Hokkaido, Japan.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster opened as an opening-day attraction at Otaru Dynalecx in 1999 as the Dyna Coaster (ダイナコースター). It featured spinning cars.

After the venue's closure, all rides, including the coaster, were relocated to the Rusutsu Resort. The ride cars were swapped out for a different set.

Since 2024, the ride has been standing but not in operation. The ride page has since been removed from Rusutsu's website.

fullText:
Mad Mouse (Japanese: マッドマウス) is a steel sit-down roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer SBF Visa Group located at Rusutsu Resort in Rusutsu, Abuta, Shiribeshi, Hokkaido, Japan.

The coaster opened as an opening-day attraction at Otaru Dynalecx in 1999 as the Dyna Coaster (ダイナコースター). It featured spinning cars.

After the venue's closure, all rides, including the coaster, were relocated to the Rusutsu Resort. The ride cars were swapped out for a different set.

Since 2024, the ride has been standing but not in operation. The ride page has since been removed from Rusutsu's website.
